title: WIP - dns: prepend dns server to resolv.conf

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

The reason we have the current behavior is to match Docker, which I think
we want to continue.

If we want to retain existing ones, we probably want a special sigil to add
the ones already present, and then you can keep using the DNS flag to add
more.

On Fri, Aug 9, 2019, 04:36 OpenShift CI Robot <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCDGHMMMJ5E6TJJG2Q3QDUUAHANCNFSM4IKR2LUQ>
> .
>

Prepend a + or append a +?
--dns +dns.redhat.com      -> Preppend
--dns dns.redhat.com        -> Replace
--dns dns.redhat.com+     -> Append

> Prepend a + or append a +?
> --dns +dns.redhat.com -> Preppend
> --dns dns.redhat.com -> Replace
> --dns dns.redhat.com+ -> Append

This sounds good! I think it would also be nice to make this configurable in the `libpod.conf`, so users don't have to add it manually all the time (assuming a consistent/stable networking env).
It sounds like it would be a good candidate for the nonexistent `podman.conf`

> It sounds like it would be a good candidate for the nonexistent `podman.conf`

We can split it in two PRs but why not use `libpod.conf` for it?
Ehh, Dan has made it clear we don't want a `podman.conf`, but I still see cases like this and think that they're better candidates for a config file specific to Podman, and not a global one.

Well up to now we have been relying on Environment variables.  My problem with config files for specific tools is that we start to make interacting between the tools more difficult.  

IE Does `buildah bud --dns` follow the rules for podman.conf how about `podman build --dns`?

> Prepend a + or append a +?
> --dns +dns.redhat.com -> Preppend
> --dns dns.redhat.com -> Replace
> --dns dns.redhat.com+ -> Append

@baude, are you cool with that? I want to make sure that's not crossing plans of your networking work.
@baude @rhatdan ... let's chat about this PR today as a PS.

@baude, @rhatdan, yesterday, we were thinking about suggesting to mount the host's resolv.conf but  changes to the host's resolv.conf are not propagated. It's not obvious to me _why_ since I commented out the default bind-mount creation; other files are propagated correctly.

One way or another, it doesn't help with the issue. I think we can either follow the idea of this PR or suggest to specify multiple dns servers are container creation.

Might want to ask Toolbox how they do it.

I'm not strictly opposed to allowing DNS prepend, but it needs to be optional.
Well if you want to go forward with the prepent/append, I guess I can live with it.  As long as --dns foobar continues the replace.

@mheon, the only way I see we could support the prepend/append/replace logic is to extend the container config with two new fields for prepended/appended servers. Changing the DNSServer field would break backward compat.

This seems ugly enough to me to drop the idea. WDYT?

I think you're right about implementation, given we store as net.IP instead of string.

If you feel like going forward, I'm not opposed to adding a field to the DB that would handle prepend/append (I think you can get away with one, if you store the IP and where to put it together), but managing compatibility with the existing DB field could be complex... So I tend to agree that this isn't worth the complexity.
